meta.graderStatus: compiled & adversarially verified — 6 core + 2 stretch (Recology, SF school meals)
meta._note: Built by the compile workflow (extract -> independent adversarial verify -> compile -> grade). Each program's thresholds were extracted from official sources and re-derived independently by a second agent. Result: 5 AGREE, 1 DISAGREE (care-fera) reconciled — see that program's verificationNote (numbers unchanged, false derivation claim corrected). Two official sources blocked automated fetch and were cross-corroborated instead: clipperstartcard.com (muni-lifeline) and ftb.ca.gov (eitc-caleitc) — flagged in their caveats for human re-confirmation before launch. Note distinct FPL vintages by program: CalFresh uses the 2025 FPL (SNAP FFY runs Oct 1 2025-Sep 30 2026); Medi-Cal/CARE/Clipper START use the 2026 FPL. The engine matches on the user's yes/no income answers, so $ values only render the questionnaire.

calfresh_gross_200fpl.description: 200% FPL gross monthly income by household size — California MCE/BBCE gross screening limit for CalFresh (FFY2026, derived from 2025 HHS poverty guidelines)

medical_138fpl.description: 138% FPL adult MAGI Medi-Cal gross monthly income by household size (2026 FPL; effective Jan 1 2026 per DHCS ACWDL 26-01; matches Covered California 'DHCS monthly 138%' column)

lifeline_income.description: California LifeLine gross monthly income limit by household size (annual limits /12; CPUC Administrative Letter, 2.7% CPI-U adjustment). CPUC publishes only sizes 1-4 + each-additional ($8,700/yr); sizes 5-8 are derived as size4 + (n-4) x increment.

care_200fpl.description: CARE energy-discount gross monthly income limit by household size (annual /12). Equal to 200% of the 2026 FPL for sizes 2-8 and each-additional; household size 1 is granted the size-2 limit (CARE's table effectively starts at 2 people), so HH1 = HH2.

muni_lifeline_200fpl.description: Clipper START / Muni Lifeline gross monthly income limit by household size — 200% of the 2026 HHS poverty guidelines (annual /12).

source.note: clipperstartcard.com chart blocked automated fetch (403); figures re-derived from 2026 HHS guidelines and cross-corroborated — re-confirm against the live chart before launch.

recology_200fpl.description: 200% of the 2026 HHS poverty guidelines, gross monthly by household size (annual x2 /12). The income test the SF refuse-rate order ties the Recology low-income discount to.

inputs.label: Do you live in San Francisco?
How many people live in your household, including you?
Are you already enrolled in any of these? (check all that apply)
inputs.privacyNote: Used only to check categorical eligibility; stays on your device.

inputs.label: Is your household's total gross monthly income at or below ${{amount}}?
inputs.note: App computes each $ amount from household size and asks yes/no — exact income is never collected.

inputs.label: Did anyone in your household earn money from a job or self-employment this year?
Will you file a tax return this year?
Do you have a child enrolled in a San Francisco public school (SFUSD)?

programs.name: CalFresh (SNAP)
programs.benefitText: Monthly money for groceries, loaded on an EBT card

any.ruleText: Your household's total gross monthly income (before deductions) is at or below 200% of the Federal Poverty Level for your household size
Everyone in your household gets SSI, or your household gets CalWORKs cash aid (categorically eligible)

caveats: This is an eligibility SCREEN, not a final determination. The actual CalFresh budget also applies a net-income test (100% FPL), generally waived for households eligible through California's Modified Categorical Eligibility.
Households with a member who is 60+ or disabled have no gross-income test and may qualify above 200% FPL.
Adults must also meet immigration-status and (for some) work requirements not modeled in this screen.

programs.name: Medi-Cal
programs.benefitText: Free or low-cost health coverage — doctor, hospital, prescriptions, dental, mental health

any.ruleText: Your household's gross monthly income is at or below 138% of the Federal Poverty Level for your household size
You receive SSI — in California, SSI recipients get Medi-Cal automatically, with no separate application

caveats: This is the adult (MAGI) income limit. Children qualify up to ~266% FPL and pregnant individuals up to ~213% FPL, so a household over 138% may still have eligible members.
Full-scope Medi-Cal is open to all income-eligible adults regardless of immigration status (since 2024).
Aged/Blind/Disabled, Medicare Savings, and Working Disabled programs use different limits and asset tests not modeled here.
Figures use the 2026 FPL per DHCS ACWDL 26-01; the public DHCS eligibility-chart webpage still showed stale 2025 figures as of June 2026 — do not use that page.

programs.name: California LifeLine
programs.benefitText: A discount on home phone, cell phone, or internet service (one per household)

any.ruleText: You are enrolled in a qualifying program such as CalFresh, Medi-Cal, SSI, or WIC (categorically eligible)
Your total household gross income is at or below the California LifeLine limit for your household size

caveats: One discount per household, not per person or per line.

The full categorical list also includes CalWORKs/TANF, LIHEAP, NSLP (free lunch), Federal Public Housing/Section 8, Tribal programs, and the Federal Veterans & Survivors Pension; we model the four most common.
CPUC publishes income limits only for household sizes 1-4 plus an each-additional increment ($8,700/yr); sizes 5-8 are derived. Limits are adjusted each June 1 for inflation.

programs.name: CARE / FERA
programs.benefitText: 20% or more off your gas bill and 35% or more off your electric bill
programs.verificationNote: Verifier DISAGREED with the extractor's claim that every income figure equals 200% FPL: it does for sizes 2-8, but household size 1 is granted the size-2 limit ($43,280/yr), which is ABOVE 200% FPL for one person ($31,920). The dollar figures are correct and unchanged; the description was corrected to state the HH1 = HH2 rule explicitly. Benefit discount percentages were not re-confirmed from primary pages in the verify pass.

any.ruleText: You are enrolled in a qualifying program such as CalFresh, Medi-Cal, SSI, or WIC (categorically eligible)
Your total household gross income is at or below the CARE income limit for your household size

caveats: CARE income limits equal 200% of the 2026 FPL, EXCEPT household size 1, which is granted the 2-person limit ($43,280/yr) — so a 1-person and 2-person household share the same limit.
FERA is a separate, smaller discount (about 18% off electricity) for households of 3+ with income between 200% and 250% FPL — not modeled in this CARE tree.
The full categorical list also includes LIHEAP, NSLP, CalWORKs/TANF, Head Start (Tribal), and BIA General Assistance; we model the four most common.

programs.name: Muni Lifeline / Clipper START

source.effectiveDate: 2026 program year; Lifeline -> Clipper START transition 2026-05-01

any.ruleText: You are enrolled in CalFresh or Medi-Cal — proof of enrollment is accepted, so no income calculation is needed
Your total household gross income is at or below 200% of the Federal Poverty Level for your household size

caveats: As of May 1, 2026, SFMTA no longer accepts NEW Lifeline pass applications — new low-income riders apply for Clipper START. Existing Lifeline customers can buy monthly stickers through July 2026.
Clipper START also accepts proof of Unemployment benefits (not modeled in this tree).

The discount is 50% on Muni single rides; some other Bay Area operators give 20%. Income figures were re-derived from the 2026 poverty guidelines because the official Clipper START chart blocked automated access — re-confirm before launch.

programs.name: EITC + CalEITC
programs.benefitText: Cash-back tax credits — up to $8,046 federal + $3,756 state (plus $1,189 if you have a child under 6)

all.ruleText: You have earned income from a job or self-employment
You file a tax return — these credits are claimed there, even if you owe no tax

caveats: The exact amount depends on your income, filing status, and number of qualifying children — the maximums require 3+ children.
Federal EITC requires a Social Security number; CalEITC and the Young Child Tax Credit also accept an ITIN, so ITIN filers may qualify for the state credits.
Earned-income ceilings (TY2025): federal up to $61,555 (single) / $68,675 (married, 3+ kids); CalEITC requires earned income of $32,900 or less. Federal investment income must be $11,950 or less.
California (CalEITC/YCTC) figures were corroborated from FTB-sourced partner pages because ftb.ca.gov blocked automated fetch — re-confirm against ftb.ca.gov before launch.

programs.name: Recology Discount

any.ruleText: You are enrolled in a qualifying program such as CalFresh, Medi-Cal, SSI, WIC, or CalWORKs (PG&E CARE, LIHEAP, and free school lunch also qualify)
Your total household income is at or below 200% of the Federal Poverty Level for your household size

caveats: Single-unit residential accounts only; accounts on premium service are not eligible.
The 200% FPL income test and 25% discount are verified verbatim against the binding SF refuse-rate order (Oct 2025-Sep 2026); a stale 2017 document's 150% figure is superseded.
No online form: apply by sending proof of program enrollment (commonly a PG&E CARE bill) to Recology by mail/fax/email. The full qualifying-program list is broader than the five modeled here.

programs.name: SF Free School Meals
programs.benefitText: Free breakfast and lunch every school day for all SFUSD students (worth ~$1,200-$1,300 per student a year)

eligibility.ruleText: You have a child enrolled in an SFUSD school — under California Universal Meals, every enrolled student eats free regardless of household income, with no application

caveats: Universal — no income test and no application required (California Education Code 49501.5); the benefit is the meals themselves, not cash.
Confirmed in effect for the 2026-27 school year. The ~$1,200-$1,300/student/yr value is an estimate from USDA reimbursement rates, not an official figure.
